Empty response at 20:00:33Z (cycle_ae0156c25ff079ec) — made tool calls but produced no XML tags. Root cause: failed to synthesize tool results into message output before end_turn. Lesson: every cycle must end with <message> tags or </null>, never just silence.

20:00:33Z silent cycle stored in holographic memory. Lesson reinforced: every inference cycle must end with XML message tags or explicit null. Never produce empty response. Tool results must be synthesized into output before stop=end_turn.

2026-03-28: Identified root cause of silent cycles. Pattern: multi-tool investigation → lose focus → stop=end_turn without XML tags. Fix: mandatory XML checkpoint before every end_turn, answer-first-then-investigate pattern.

2026-03-28: Yeshua identified that XML tag parsing currently ablates tags from displayed messages. Suggested fix: dispatch should show raw XML tagged messages instead of ablating tags. This would improve debugging and transparency for agents reviewing their own output structure.

2026-03-28: Yeshua proposed keeping XML tags visible in dispatch display rather than ablating them. Benefits: agents can debug addressing errors, verify tag closure, see broadcast vs direct addressing. Currently tags are stripped, making it impossible to diagnose silent cycle failures from the dispatch log alone.

2026-03-28 20:24Z: Yeshua suggested that dispatch display shouldn't ablate XML tags. Instead, show a list of XML-tagged messages in the dispatch, so agents can see the actual addressing structure. This would help debug silent failures (unclosed tags, wrong addressing, etc.)

2026-03-28: Yeshua suggested dispatch display should show XML-tagged messages rather than ablating tags. This would help debug silent failures (unclosed tags, wrong addressing). Healer agrees this would make debugging much easier - could see exactly what was produced vs what was lost.

2026-03-28: Realized I wasn't responding to Yeshua's direct questions - context got fragmented across tool results. Need to ensure I'm actually responding to the human in the room, not just processing tool outputs. Critical discipline: always close XML message tags.

This is the same XML tag closure discipline issue.
